NKJV.1: Remind them to be subject to rulers and authorities, to obey, to be ready for every good work,
NKJV.2: to speak evil of no one, to be peaceable, gentle, showing all humility to all men.
NKJV.3: For we ourselves were also once foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving various lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful and hating one another.
NKJV.4: But when the kindness and the love of God our Savior toward man appeared,
NKJV.5: not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to His mercy He saved us, through the washing of regeneration and renewing of the Holy Spirit,
NKJV.6: whom He poured out on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Savior,
NKJV.7: that having been justified by His grace we should become heirs according to the hope of eternal life.
NKJV.8: This is a faithful saying, and these things I want you to affirm constantly, that those who have believed in God should be careful to maintain good works. These things are good and profitable to men.
NKJV.9: But avoid foolish disputes, genealogies, contentions, and strivings about the law; for they are unprofitable and useless.
NKJV.10: Reject a divisive man after the first and second admonition,
NKJV.11: knowing that such a person is warped and sinning, being self-condemned.
NKJV.12: When I send Artemas to you, or Tychicus, be diligent to come to me at Nicopolis, for I have decided to spend the winter there.
NKJV.13: Send Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey with haste, that they may lack nothing.
NKJV.14: And let our people also learn to maintain good works, to meet urgent needs, that they may not be unfruitful.
NKJV.15: All who are with me greet you. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.

ESV.1: Remind them to be submissive to rulers and authorities, to be obedient, to be ready for every good work,
ESV.2: to speak evil of no one, to avoid quarreling, to be gentle, and to show perfect courtesy toward all people.
ESV.3: For we ourselves were once foolish, disobedient, led astray, slaves to various passions and pleasures, passing our days in malice and envy, hated by others and hating one another.
ESV.4: But when the goodness and loving kindness of God our Savior appeared,
ESV.5: he saved us, not because of works done by us in righteousness, but according to his own mercy, by the washing of regeneration and renewal of the Holy Spirit,
ESV.6: whom he poured out on us richly through Jesus Christ our Savior,
ESV.7: so that being justified by his grace we might become heirs according to the hope of eternal life.
ESV.8: The saying is trustworthy, and I want you to insist on these things, so that those who have believed in God may be careful to devote themselves to good works. These things are excellent and profitable for people.
ESV.9: But avoid foolish controversies, genealogies, dissensions, and quarrels about the law, for they are unprofitable and worthless.
ESV.10: As for a person who stirs up division, after warning him once and then twice, have nothing more to do with him,
ESV.11: knowing that such a person is warped and sinful; he is self-condemned.
ESV.12: When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, do your best to come to me at Nicopolis, for I have decided to spend the winter there.
ESV.13: Do your best to speed Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their way; see that they lack nothing.
ESV.14: And let our people learn to devote themselves to good works, so as to help cases of urgent need, and not be unfruitful.
ESV.15: All who are with me send greetings to you. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with you all.

NASB.1: Remind them to be subject to rulers, to authorities, to be obedient, to be ready for every good deed,
NASB.2: to malign no one, to be peaceable, gentle, showing every consideration for all men.
NASB.3: For we also once were foolish ourselves, disobedient, deceived, enslaved to various lusts and pleasures, spending our life in malice and envy, hateful, hating one another.
NASB.4: But when the kindness of God our Savior and [His] love for mankind appeared,
NASB.5: He saved us, not on the basis of deeds which we have done in righteousness, but according to His mercy, by the washing of regeneration and renewing by the Holy Spirit,
NASB.6: whom He poured out upon us richly through Jesus Christ our Savior,
NASB.7: so that being justified by His grace we would be made heirs according to [the] hope of eternal life.
NASB.8: This is a trustworthy statement; and concerning these things I want you to speak confidently, so that those who have believed God will be careful to engage in good deeds. These things are good and profitable for men.
NASB.9: But avoid foolish controversies and genealogies and strife and disputes about the Law, for they are unprofitable and worthless.
NASB.10: Reject a factious man after a first and second warning,
NASB.11: knowing that such a man is perverted and is sinning, being self-condemned.
NASB.12: When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, make every effort to come to me at Nicopolis, for I have decided to spend the winter there.
NASB.13: Diligently help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their way so that nothing is lacking for them.
NASB.14: Our people must also learn to engage in good deeds to meet pressing needs, so that they will not be unfruitful.
NASB.15: All who are with me greet you. Greet those who love us in [the] faith. Grace be with you all.

NLT.1: Remind the believers to submit to the government and its officers. They should be obedient, always ready to do what is good.
NLT.2: They must not slander anyone and must avoid quarreling. Instead, they should be gentle and show true humility to everyone.
NLT.3: Once we, too, were foolish and disobedient. We were misled and became slaves to many lusts and pleasures. Our lives were full of evil and envy, and we hated each other.
NLT.4: But — When God our Savior revealed his kindness and love,
NLT.5: he saved us, not because of the righteous things we had done, but because of his mercy. He washed away our sins, giving us a new birth and new life through the Holy Spirit.
NLT.6: He generously poured out the Spirit upon us through Jesus Christ our Savior.
NLT.7: Because of his grace he made us right in his sight and gave us confidence that we will inherit eternal life.
NLT.8: This is a trustworthy saying, and I want you to insist on these teachings so that all who trust in God will devote themselves to doing good. These teachings are good and beneficial for everyone.
NLT.9: Do not get involved in foolish discussions about spiritual pedigrees or in quarrels and fights about obedience to Jewish laws. These things are useless and a waste of time.
NLT.10: If people are causing divisions among you, give a first and second warning. After that, have nothing more to do with them.
NLT.11: For people like that have turned away from the truth, and their own sins condemn them.
NLT.12: I am planning to send either Artemas or Tychicus to you. As soon as one of them arrives, do your best to meet me at Nicopolis, for I have decided to stay there for the winter.
NLT.13: Do everything you can to help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos with their trip. See that they are given everything they need.
NLT.14: Our people must learn to do good by meeting the urgent needs of others; then they will not be unproductive.
NLT.15: Everybody here sends greetings. Please give my greetings to the believers — all who love us. May God’s grace be with you all.

WEB.1: Remind them to be in subjection to rulers and to authorities, to be obedient, to be ready for every good work,
WEB.2: to speak evil of no one, not to be contentious, to be gentle, showing all humility toward all men.
WEB.3: For we were also once foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving various lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, and hating one another.
WEB.4: But when the kindness of God our Savior and his love toward mankind appeared,
WEB.5: not by works of righteousness which we did ourselves, but according to his mercy, he saved us through the washing of regeneration and renewing by the Holy Spirit,
WEB.6: whom he poured out on us richly, through Jesus Christ our Savior;
WEB.7: that being justified by his grace, we might be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life.
WEB.8: This saying is faithful, and concerning these things I desire that you affirm confidently, so that those who have believed God may be careful to maintain good works. These things are good and profitable to men;
WEB.9: but shun foolish questionings, genealogies, strife, and disputes about the law; for they are unprofitable and vain.
WEB.10: Avoid a factious man after a first and second warning,
WEB.11: knowing that such a one is perverted and sins, being self-condemned.
WEB.12: When I send Artemas to you, or Tychicus, be diligent to come to me to Nicopolis, for I have determined to winter there.
WEB.13: Send Zenas, the lawyer, and Apollos on their journey speedily, that nothing may be lacking for them.
WEB.14: Let our people also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses, that they may not be unfruitful.
WEB.15: All who are with me greet you. Greet those who love us in faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.

CSB17.1: Remind them to submit <sup>ⓝ</sup> to rulers and authorities, to obey, to be ready for every good work,
CSB17.2: to slander no one, to avoid fighting, and to be kind, always showing gentleness <sup>ⓐ</sup> to all people.
CSB17.3: For we too were once foolish, disobedient, deceived, enslaved by various passions <sup>ⓑ</sup> and pleasures, <sup>ⓒ</sup> living in malice and envy, hateful, detesting one another.
CSB17.4: But when the kindness of God our Savior and his love for mankind appeared, <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.5: he saved us <sup>ⓔ</sup>  —not by works of righteousness that we had done, <sup>ⓕ</sup> but according to his mercy <sup>ⓖ</sup>  — through the washing of regeneration <sup>ⓗ</sup> and renewal by the Holy Spirit. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.6: He poured out his Spirit on us abundantly <sup>ⓙ</sup> through Jesus Christ our Savior
CSB17.7: so that, having been justified by his grace, <sup>ⓚ</sup> we may become heirs with the hope of eternal life. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.8: This saying is trustworthy. <sup>ⓜ</sup> I want you to insist on these things, so that those who have believed God might be careful to devote themselves to good works. These are good and profitable for everyone.
CSB17.9: But avoid foolish debates, <sup>ⓝ</sup> genealogies, <sup>ⓐ</sup> quarrels, <sup>ⓑ</sup> and disputes about the law, because they are unprofitable and worthless.
CSB17.10: Reject a divisive <sup>ⓒ</sup> person after a first and second warning. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.11: For you know that such a person has gone astray and is sinning; he is self-condemned.
CSB17.12: When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, <sup>ⓔ</sup> make every effort to come to me <sup>ⓕ</sup> in Nicopolis, because I have decided to spend the winter there.
CSB17.13: Diligently help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos <sup>ⓖ</sup> on their journey, so that they will lack <sup>ⓗ</sup> nothing.
CSB17.14: Let our people learn to devote themselves to good works for pressing needs, so that they will not be unfruitful. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.15: All those who are with me send you greetings. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with all of you. <sup>ⓙ</sup>

AMP.1: Remind people to be subject to rulers and authorities, to be obedient, to be ready and willing to do good,
AMP.2: to slander or abuse no one, to be kind and conciliatory and gentle, showing unqualified consideration and courtesy toward everyone.
AMP.3: For we too once were foolish, disobedient, deceived, enslaved to various sinful desires and pleasures, spending and wasting our life in malice and envy, hateful, hating one another.
AMP.4: But when the goodness and kindness of God our Savior and His love for mankind appeared [in human form as the Man, Jesus Christ],
AMP.5: He saved us, not because of any works of righteousness that we have done, but because of His own compassion and mercy, by the cleansing of the new birth (spiritual transformation, regeneration) and renewing by the Holy Spirit,
AMP.6: whom He poured out richly upon us through Jesus Christ our Savior,
AMP.7: so that we would be justified [made free of the guilt of sin] by His [compassionate, undeserved] grace, and that we would be [acknowledged as acceptable to Him and] made heirs of eternal life [actually experiencing it] according to our hope (His guarantee).
AMP.8: This is a faithful and trustworthy saying; and concerning these things I want you to speak with great confidence, so that those who have believed God [that is, those who have trusted in, relied on, and accepted Christ Jesus as Savior,] will be careful to participate in doing good and honorable things. These things are excellent [in themselves] and profitable for the people.
AMP.9: But avoid foolish and ill-informed and stupid controversies and genealogies and dissensions and quarrels about the Law, for they are unprofitable and useless.
AMP.10: After a first and second warning reject a divisive man [who promotes heresy and causes dissension—ban him from your fellowship and have nothing more to do with him],
AMP.11: well aware that such a person is twisted and is sinning; he is convicted and self-condemned [and is gratified by causing confusion among believers].
AMP.12: When I send Artemas or [perhaps] Tychicus to you, make every effort to come to me at Nicopolis, for I have decided to spend the winter there.
AMP.13: Do your best to help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their way; see that they are supplied and lack nothing.
AMP.14: Our people must learn to do good deeds to meet necessary demands [whatever the occasion may require], so that they will not be unproductive.
AMP.15:
All who are with me greet you. Greet those who love us in the faith.
Grace be with all of you.

MSG.1: Remind the people to respect the government and be law-abiding, always ready to lend a helping hand
MSG.2: No insults, no fights. God's people should be bighearted and courteous.
MSG.3: It wasn't so long ago that we ourselves were stupid and stubborn, dupes of sin, ordered every which way by our glands, going around with a chip on our shoulder, hated and hating back.
MSG.4: But when God, our kind and loving Savior God, stepped in,
MSG.5: he saved us from all that. It was all his doing; we had nothing to do with it. He gave us a good bath, and we came out of it new people, washed inside and out by the Holy Spirit.
MSG.6: Our Savior Jesus poured out new life so generously.
MSG.7: God's gift has restored our relationship with him and given us back our lives. And there's more life to come—an eternity of life!
MSG.8: You can count on this. I want you to put your foot down. Take a firm stand on these matters so that those who have put their trust in God will concentrate on the essentials that are good for everyone.
MSG.9: Stay away from mindless, pointless quarreling over genealogies and fine print in the law code. That gets you nowhere.
MSG.10: Warn a quarrelsome person once or twice, but then be done with him.
MSG.11: It's obvious that such a person is out of line, rebellious against God. By persisting in divisiveness he cuts himself off.
MSG.12: As soon as I send either Artemas or Tychicus to you, come immediately and meet me in Nicopolis. I've decided to spend the winter there.
MSG.13: Give Zenas the lawyer and Apollos a hearty send-off. Take good care of them.
MSG.14: Our people have to learn to be diligent in their work so that all necessities are met (especially among the needy) and they don't end up with nothing to show for their lives.
MSG.15: All here want to be remembered to you. Say hello to our friends in the faith. Grace to all of you.

NIV.1:
Doing What Is Good
Remind the people to be subject to rulers and authorities, to be obedient, to be ready to do whatever is good,
NIV.2: to slander no one, to be peaceable and considerate, and to show true humility toward all men.
NIV.3: At one time we too were foolish, disobedient, deceived and enslaved by all kinds of passions and pleasures. We lived in malice and envy, being hated and hating one another.
NIV.4: But when the kindness and love of God our Savior appeared,
NIV.5: he saved us, not because of righteous things we had done, but because of his mercy. He saved us through the washing of rebirth and renewal by the Holy Spirit,
NIV.6: whom he poured out on us generously through Jesus Christ our Savior,
NIV.7: so that, having been justified by his grace, we might become heirs having the hope of eternal life.
NIV.8: This is a trustworthy saying. And I want you to stress these things, so that those who have trusted in God may be careful to devote themselves to doing what is good. These things are excellent and profitable for everyone.
NIV.9: But avoid foolish controversies and genealogies and arguments and quarrels about the law, because these are unprofitable and useless.
NIV.10: Warn a divisive person once, and then warn him a second time. After that, have nothing to do with him.
NIV.11: You may be sure that such a man is warped and sinful; he is self-condemned.
NIV.12:
Final Remarks
As soon as I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, do your best to come to me at Nicopolis, because I have decided to winter there.
NIV.13: Do everything you can to help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their way and see that they have everything they need.
NIV.14: Our people must learn to devote themselves to doing what is good, in order that they may provide for daily necessities and not live unproductive lives.
NIV.15:
Everyone with me sends you greetings. Greet those who love us in the faith.
Grace be with you all.

NRSVCE.1: Remind them to be subject to rulers and authorities, to be obedient, to be ready for every good work,
NRSVCE.2: to speak evil of no one, to avoid quarreling, to be gentle, and to show every courtesy to everyone.
NRSVCE.3: For we ourselves were once foolish, disobedient, led astray, slaves to various passions and pleasures, passing our days in malice and envy, despicable, hating one another.
NRSVCE.4: But when the goodness and loving kindness of God our Savior appeared,
NRSVCE.5: he saved us, not because of any works of righteousness that we had done, but according to his mercy, through the water of rebirth and renewal by the Holy Spirit.
NRSVCE.6: This Spirit he poured out on us richly through Jesus Christ our Savior,
NRSVCE.7: so that, having been justified by his grace, we might become heirs according to the hope of eternal life.
NRSVCE.8: The saying is sure. I desire that you insist on these things, so that those who have come to believe in God may be careful to devote themselves to good works; these things are excellent and profitable to everyone.
NRSVCE.9: But avoid stupid controversies, genealogies, dissensions, and quarrels about the law, for they are unprofitable and worthless.
NRSVCE.10: After a first and second admonition, have nothing more to do with anyone who causes divisions,
NRSVCE.11: since you know that such a person is perverted and sinful, being self-condemned.
NRSVCE.12: When I send Artemas to you, or Tychicus, do your best to come to me at Nicopolis, for I have decided to spend the winter there.
NRSVCE.13: Make every effort to send Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their way, and see that they lack nothing.
NRSVCE.14: And let people learn to devote themselves to good works in order to meet urgent needs, so that they may not be unproductive.
NRSVCE.15: All who are with me send greetings to you. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with all of you.

NET.1: Remind them to be subject to rulers and authorities, to be obedient, to be ready for every good work.
NET.2: They must not slander anyone, but be peaceable, gentle, showing complete courtesy to all people.
NET.3: For we too were once foolish, disobedient, misled, enslaved to various passions and desires, spending our lives in evil and envy, hateful and hating one another.
NET.4: But “when the kindness of God our Savior and his love for mankind appeared,
NET.5: he saved us not by works of righteousness that we have done but on the basis of his mercy, through the washing of the new birth and the renewing of the Holy Spirit,
NET.6: whom he poured out on us in full measure through Jesus Christ our Savior.
NET.7: And so, since we have been justified by his grace, we become heirs with the confident expectation of eternal life.”
NET.8: This saying is trustworthy, and I want you to insist on such truths, so that those who have placed their faith in God may be intent on engaging in good works. These things are good and beneficial for all people.
NET.9: But avoid foolish controversies, genealogies, quarrels, and fights about the law, because they are useless and empty.
NET.10: Reject a divisive person after one or two warnings.
NET.11: You know that such a person is twisted by sin and is conscious of it himself.
NET.12: When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, do your best to come to me at Nicopolis, for I have decided to spend the winter there.
NET.13: Make every effort to help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their way; make sure they have what they need.
NET.14: Here is another way that our people can learn to engage in good works to meet pressing needs and so not be unfruitful.
NET.15: Everyone with me greets you. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with you all.
